name: Employment
description: This layer shows hours worked, and those unemployed and not in labor force. This is shown by tract, county, and state boundaries. This service is updated annually to contain the most currently released American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year data, and contains estimates and margins of error. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis.  <div><br /></div><div>This layer is symbolized to show the percentage of unemployed population within the civilian labor force. To see the full list of attributes available in this service, go to the &quot;Data&quot; tab, and choose &quot;Fields&quot; at the top right. <div><br /></div><div><div><b>Current Vintage</b>: <span style="font-family:&quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size:16px;">2019-2023</span></div><div><b>ACS Table(s)</b>: B23020, B23025</div><div><b>Data downloaded from</b>: <a>Census Bureau's API for American Community Survey</a> </div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>The United States Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS):</b></div><div><ul><li><a>About the Survey</a><br /></li><li><a>Geography &amp; ACS</a><br /></li><li><a>Technical Documentation</a><br /></li><li><a>News &amp; Updates</a><br /></li></ul><div>This ready-to-use layer can be used within ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, its configurable apps, dashboards, Story Maps, custom apps, and mobile apps. Data can also be exported for offline workflows. For more information about ACS layers, visit the <a>FAQ</a>. Please cite the Census and ACS when using this data.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div><b>Data Note from the Census:</b><br /></div><div>Data are based on a sample and are subject to sampling variability. The degree of uncertainty for an estimate arising from sampling variability is represented through the use of a margin of error. The value shown here is the 90 percent margin of error. The margin of error can be interpreted as providing a 90 percent probability that the interval defined by the estimate minus the margin of error and the estimate plus the margin of error (the lower and upper confidence bounds) contains the true value. In addition to sampling variability, the ACS estimates are subject to nonsampling error (for a discussion of nonsampling variability, see <a>Accuracy of the Data</a>).  The effect of nonsampling error is not represented in these tables.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><b>Data Processing Notes:</b></div><div><ul><li>This layer is updated automatically when the most current vintage of ACS data is released each year, usually in December. The layer always contains the latest available ACS 5-year estimates. It is updated annually within days of the Census Bureau's release schedule. <a>Click here</a> to learn more about ACS data releases.<br /></li><li>Boundaries come from the <a>US Census TIGER geodatabases,</a> specifically, the National Sub-State Geography Database (named tlgdb_(year)_a_us_substategeo.gdb). Boundaries are updated at the same time as the data updates (annually), and the boundary vintage appropriately matches the data vintage <a>as specified by the Census</a>. These are Census boundaries with water and/or coastlines erased for cartographic and mapping purposes. For census tracts, the water cutouts are derived from a subset of the <a>2020 Areal Hydrography boundaries offered by TIGER</a>. Water bodies and rivers which are 50 million square meters or larger (mid to large sized water bodies) are erased from the tract level boundaries, as well as additional important features. For state and county boundaries, the water and coastlines are derived from the coastlines of the 2022 500k <a>TIGER Cartographic Boundary Shapefiles</a>. These are erased to more accurately portray the coastlines and Great Lakes. The original AWATER and ALAND fields are still available as attributes within the data table (units are square meters).</li><li>The States layer contains 52 records - all US states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico</li><li>Census tracts with no population that occur in areas of water, such as oceans, are removed from this data service (Census Tracts beginning with 99).</li><li>Percentages and derived counts, and associated margins of error, are calculated values (that can be identified by the &quot;_calc_&quot; stub in the field name), and abide by the specifications <a>defined by the American Community Survey</a>.</li><li>Field alias names were created based on the Table Shells file available from the <a>American Community Survey Summary File Documentation page</a>.<br /></li><li>Negative values (e.g., -4444...) have been set to null, with the exception of -5555... which has been set to zero. These negative values exist in the raw API data to indicate the following situations:</li><ul><li>The margin of error column indicates that either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute a standard error and thus the margin of error. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>Either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest interval or upper interval of an open-ended distribution.<br /></li><li>The median falls in the lowest interval of an open-ended distribution, or in the upper interval of an open-ended distribution. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>The estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.<br /></li><li>The data for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small.</li></ul></ul></div></div></div>

fields.name: OBJECTID
fields.description: {"value":"An ObjectID is a unique, not null integer field used to uniquely identify rows in tables in a geographical table.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: GEOID
fields.description: {"value":"Geographic Identifier, or GEOID is the numeric code used to uniquely identify all administrative/legal and statistical geographic areas for which the Census Bureau tabulates data.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: ALAND
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of land in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: AWATER
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of water in square meters within this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"measurement"}

fields.name: NAME
fields.description: {"value":"Name of this geographical area", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: State
fields.description: {"value":"State in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: County
fields.description: {"value":"County in which this geography resides", "fieldValueType":"nameOrTitle"}

fields.name: B23020_001E
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Population Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months
fields.description: {"value":"Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23020_001M
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Population Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Mean Usual Hours Worked of Population Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months (90% confidence). Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23020_002E
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Men Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months
fields.description: {"value":"Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23020_002M
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Men Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Mean Usual Hours Worked of Men Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months (90% confidence). Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23020_003E
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Women Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months
fields.description: {"value":"Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23020_003M
fields.alias: Mean Usual Hours Worked of Women Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Mean Usual Hours Worked of Women Age 16 to 64 who Have Worked in Past 12 Months (90% confidence). Number of hours worked per week in the majority of weeks worked for pay or profit, or worked without pay on a family farm or in a family business. Weeks of active service in the Armed Forces are also included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_001E
fields.description: {"value":"Total population 16 and over includes those living in households, on active duty in the Armed Forces, and those living in group quarters such as correctional facilities, skilled nursing facilities, juvenile facilities, college dorms, etc.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_001M
fields.alias: Total Population 16 Years and Over - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Total Population 16 Years and Over (90% confidence). Total population 16 and over includes those living in households, on active duty in the Armed Forces, and those living in group quarters such as correctional facilities, skilled nursing facilities, juvenile facilities, college dorms, etc.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_002E
fields.description: {"value":"The sum of Civilian Labor Force (civilians 16 and over who were at work last week, with a job but not at work last week, or did not have a job and were actively looking for work in during the past 4 weeks and were available to start work) and Population in Armed Forces (people on active duty with the United States Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard). \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_002M
fields.alias: All Population in Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for All Population in Labor Force (90% confidence). The sum of Civilian Labor Force (civilians 16 and over who were at work last week, with a job but not at work last week, or did not have a job and were actively looking for work in during the past 4 weeks and were available to start work) and Population in Armed Forces (people on active duty with the United States Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard). \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_003E
fields.alias: All Population in Civilian Labor Force
fields.description: {"value":"All civilians 16 and over who were at work last week, with a job but not at work last week, or did not have a job and were actively looking for work in during the past 4 weeks and were available to start work. \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents. Those only doing unpaid volunteer work are not included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_003M
fields.alias: All Population in Civilian Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for All Population in Civilian Labor Force (90% confidence). All civilians 16 and over who were at work last week, with a job but not at work last week, or did not have a job and were actively looking for work in during the past 4 weeks and were available to start work. \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents. Those only doing unpaid volunteer work are not included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_004E
fields.alias: Employed Population in Civilian Labor Force
fields.description: {"value":"All civilians 16 and over who were either at work as paid employees, or worked in their own or their family's business or farm, last week; or with a job but not at work last week, that is, had jobs or businesses from which they were temporarily absent due to illness, bad weather, industrial dispute, vacation, or other personal reasons. \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents. Those only doing unpaid volunteer work are not included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_004M
fields.alias: Employed Population in Civilian Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Employed Population in Civilian Labor Force (90% confidence). All civilians 16 and over who were either at work as paid employees, or worked in their own or their family's business or farm, last week; or with a job but not at work last week, that is, had jobs or businesses from which they were temporarily absent due to illness, bad weather, industrial dispute, vacation, or other personal reasons. \"Last week\" is the calendar week preceding the date on which the respondents completed their questionnaires or were interviewed. This week is not the same for all respondents. Those only doing unpaid volunteer work are not included.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_005E
fields.alias: Unemployed Population in Civilian Labor Force
fields.description: {"value":"All civilians 16 and over who did not have a job, were actively looking for a job during the last 4 weeks, and were available to start a job. Also included are those who were waiting to be called back to a job from which they had been temporarily laid off, and were available to start work except for temporary illness.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_005M
fields.alias: Unemployed Population in Civilian Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Unemployed Population in Civilian Labor Force (90% confidence). All civilians 16 and over who did not have a job, were actively looking for a job during the last 4 weeks, and were available to start a job. Also included are those who were waiting to be called back to a job from which they had been temporarily laid off, and were available to start work except for temporary illness.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_006E
fields.description: {"value":"People on active duty with the United States Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_006M
fields.alias: Population in Armed Forces - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Population in Armed Forces (90% confidence). People on active duty with the United States Army, Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_007E
fields.alias: Population 16 Years and Over Not in Labor Force
fields.description: {"value":"Those neither working nor actively looking for work. This category consists mainly of students, homemakers, retired workers, seasonal workers interviewed in an off season who were not looking for work, institutionalized people, and people doing only incidental unpaid family work.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_007M
fields.alias: Population 16 Years and Over Not in Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Population 16 Years and Over Not in Labor Force (90% confidence). Those neither working nor actively looking for work. This category consists mainly of students, homemakers, retired workers, seasonal workers interviewed in an off season who were not looking for work, institutionalized people, and people doing only incidental unpaid family work.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B23025_calc_pctNILFE
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of those 16 years and over who are neither working nor actively looking for work. This category consists mainly of students, homemakers, retired workers, seasonal workers interviewed in an off season who were not looking for work, institutionalized people, and people doing only incidental unpaid family work. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B23025_007E / B23025_001E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B23025_calc_pctNILFM
fields.alias: Percent Not in Labor Force - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds Percent Not in Labor Force (90% confidence). Percent of those 16 years and over who are neither working nor actively looking for work. This category consists mainly of students, homemakers, retired workers, seasonal workers interviewed in an off season who were not looking for work, institutionalized people, and people doing only incidental unpaid family work. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B23025_001E)*(sqrt((B23025_007M)^2-1.0*((B23025_007E / B23025_001E)^2)*(B23025_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B23025_calc_pctUnempE
fields.description: {"value":"The unemployment rate represents the number of unemployed people (those not currently working who are actively looking for work during the past 4 weeks and are available to start work) as a percentage of the civilian labor force. For example, if the civilian labor force equals 100 people and 7 people are unemployed, then the unemployment rate would be 7 percent. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B23025_005E / B23025_003E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B23025_calc_pctUnempM
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent Unemployed (90% confidence). The unemployment rate represents the number of unemployed people (those not currently working who are actively looking for work during the past 4 weeks and are available to start work) as a percentage of the civilian labor force. For example, if the civilian labor force equals 100 people and 7 people are unemployed, then the unemployment rate would be 7 percent. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B23025_003E)*(sqrt((B23025_005M)^2-1.0*((B23025_005E / B23025_003E)^2)*(B23025_003M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: PLACEFP
fields.description: {"value":"The FIPS Code corresponding to the census designated place in which each census tract resides.","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: PLACENAME
fields.description: {"value":"The name of the census designated place in which the census tract falls.","fieldValueType":""}
